title: 9.06.2026 — Nika, Day 5
paragraphs: The morning open sea invites a deep, quiet stillness. We stared out at the horizon, listening to the static of the radio—there was no internet this far out, some 60 kilometers from the shore—exchanging only a few sparse words. And then, suddenly, the wind shifted, turning in our favor! Sasha called out, 'Get ready, we're putting up the sails.' My heart skipped a beat—man, I wanted to ask so badly, but you went ahead and suggested it yourself! And off we went! The very preparation of the sails is a pure, distinct pleasure of its own. We hoisted them, and we simply flew. We quickly outpaced the speed of our own motor and shut it down. Even though a boat engine is nothing like the roar of a turbojet, slipping into the absolute silence of sailing is a kind of pure Zen.

That was how we made our way to Hel, where the boat's owner was waiting for us. My brother drove down, hoping the crew would join him as guests, but we didn't end up going. Later, Krzysztof and I grabbed some beers. I managed to lose my passport—but that’s just trivial drama, no place for it in a proper ship's log. (I did find it later, by the way!)
